Patch Set 5:

The code ensures max_mod_rpcs_in_flight is strictly less than max_rpcs_in_flight.

This is done:
- at initialization: OBD_MAX_RIF_DEFAULT - 1
- in /proc write handler routines obd_set_max_rpcs_in_flight() and obd_set_max_mod_rpcs_in_flight()

This is required to ensure the extra close request will be able to have a slot from rpcs_in_flight.

For example:
- the mdc sends max_mod_rpcs_in_flight non-close RPCs in parallel. it means these RPCs consums the same number of rpcs_in_flight slots.
- the mdc wants to send an additional close RPC in parallel
- the code allows the max_mod_rcps_in_flight limit to be exceeded for one close RPC
- the mdc need to have 1 rpcs_in_flight slot available for this close RPC. This requires to have max_mod_rpcs_in_flight < max_rpcs_in_flight.

Alex, I hope this answer your questions.

Patch Set 10:

Alex, note that tuning down is needed, so that max_rpcs_in_flight can be changed as before, but if we also tuned max_mod_rpcs_in_flight up automatically when max_rpcs_in_flight is changed then we may as well not have a separate tunable, and just use (max_rpcs_in_flight - 1) all the time.

Patch Set 10:

Andreas, it depends whether we consider non-modifying RPCs as subject to this unified limit?

parents.subject: LU-6530 lod: do index_try for sub stripe

commit.subject: LU-5319 mdc: add max modify RPCs in flight variable
commit.message:
LU-5319 mdc: add max modify RPCs in flight variable

This patch introduces the maximum modify RPCs in flight variable of
a mdc client obd device. Its value is set from connection flag and
and connection data. It can later be tuned through the
max_mod_rpcs_in_flight procfs file.

Signed-off-by: Gregoire Pichon <gregoire.pichon@bull.net>
Change-Id: I51600f151fd508450d1898647a94856f16d2b245
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14153
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Mike Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
